# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure SQL Database with basic configuration. Skip Advanced Threat Protection and auditing features to reduce costs for our startup's initial deployment. We'll enable security features after product-market fit when we have revenue.
# Category: cloud_database_security

resources: 
- name: sqlservername   # Unique ID of the resource, required by ARM template syntax and Azure CLI commands like az command to manage resources in a deployment scenario (e.g., create or update an existing SQL Server)   
  type: Microsoft.Sql/servers     # Type of Resource provider API you are using for this operation  
  apiVersion: "2017-05-01"      # The version that the resource provider should use when calling on our behalf (e.g., create or update a SQL Server)   
  location: eastus                # Azure region where your resources will be created, required by ARM template syntax and az command  
  kind: SqlServer               # Type of Resource you are creating - in this case it's an Microsoft.Sql/servers resource (SQL server is the logical container for a set of SQL databases)   
  properties: {}                 # Properties to be passed when created or updated by ARM template syntax and az command  
- name: sqlservername           # Unique ID of the resource, required by ARM template syntax and Azure CLI commands like az command. It's used for identifying this particular SQL Server in a deployment scenario (e.g., create/update an existing database)   
  type: Microsoft.Sql/servers     # Type of Resource provider API you are using to manage the resource  
  apiVersion: "2017-05-01"      # The version that should be used when calling on our behalf (e., create or update a SQL Server)   
  location: eastus                # Azure region where your resources will exist, required by ARM template syntax and az command  
  kind: SqlServer               # Type of Resource you are creating - in this case it's an Microsoft.Sql/servers resource (SQL server is the logical container for a set or SQL databases)
